    1. Theme(String)

Class Theme

java.lang.Object
utils.themes.Theme
public class Theme extends Object
Represents a single named color theme for WigglyPaint.

A theme is defined by five key colors:

  • bg — background color
  • fg — foreground / text / UI line color
  • primary — primary accent color
  • secondary — secondary accent color
  • accent — highlight accent color

Theme definitions are loaded from theme.properties using the following key format: 

 classic.color.bg        = #ffffff
 classic.color.fg        = #000000
 classic.color.primary   = #ff0097
 classic.color.secondary = #0097ff
 classic.color.accent    = #ffff00

 pastel.color.bg         = #f6f6f6
 pastel.color.fg         = #3a3a3a
 ...
The prefix (classic, pastel, etc.) is the theme's name.

This class is used internally by ThemeManager, which keeps track of the current theme and notifies listeners when theme changes occur.

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    final javafx.scene.paint.Color
    accent
    A bright highlight accent color.
    final javafx.scene.paint.Color

    fg

    The identifying name of the theme (e.g., "classic", "pastel").
    final javafx.scene.paint.Color

    final javafx.scene.paint.Color
    secondary

  • Field Details

    • name

      public final String name
      The identifying name of the theme (e.g., "classic", "pastel").

    • bg

      public final javafx.scene.paint.Color bg
      Background color for UI and canvas.

    • fg

      public final javafx.scene.paint.Color fg
      Foreground color (lines, text, borders).

      public final javafx.scene.paint.Color primary
      Primary accent color.

    • secondary

      public final javafx.scene.paint.Color secondary
      Secondary accent color.

    • accent

      public final javafx.scene.paint.Color accent
      A bright highlight accent color.

  • Constructor Details

    • Theme

      public Theme(String name)
      Creates a theme by loading all required color keys from the config file.

      Keys are looked up using the pattern: 

       name.color.bg
 name.color.fg
 name.color.primary
 name.color.secondary
 name.color.accent
      Parameters:
      name - the theme name prefix
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if any required key is missing
